| MCP:MGLy8W | # Spring Boot Template
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | A production-ready template for Spring Boot projects with modern best practices and built-in infrastructure.
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ![Docker Compose](https://imgur.com/qWLYDrg.png)
| MCP:MGLy8W | ![Swagger UI](https://imgur.com/5MixfZe.png)
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ## Features
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | > Only describe the production environment and ignore test or parallel environment setup details.
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | - **Spring Boot 4.0.0** (Java 25)
| MCP:MGLy8W | - **UUID Primary Keys** via Hibernate `@UuidGenerator`
| MCP:MGLy8W | - **JWT Authentication & Authorization** with refresh tokens
| MCP:MGLy8W | - **Role-based Security** with Spring Security
| MCP:MGLy8W | - **Spring Boot Actuator** for health, info, and metrics endpoints
| MCP:MGLy8W | - **Request Correlation ID** — automatic `X-Correlation-ID` header propagation and MDC logging
| MCP:MGLy8W | - **Structured JSON Logging** — human-readable console + JSON file output (Logstash format)
| MCP:MGLy8W | - **Pagination** — built into `BaseService` and controllers via Spring Data `Pageable`
| MCP:MGLy8W | - **Rate Limiting** — annotation-based (`@RateLimit`, `@RateLimitCategory`) with configurable categories
| MCP:MGLy8W | - **Idempotency** — `@Idempotent` annotation with Redis-backed request deduplication
| MCP:MGLy8W | - **Swagger UI** for API documentation ([http://localhost:8080/api/v1/swagger-ui/index.html](http://localhost:8080/api/v1/swagger-ui/index.html))
| MCP:MGLy8W | - **PostgreSQL**, **Redis**, **Kafka & Zookeeper** — all containerized via Docker Compose
| MCP:MGLy8W | - **Multi-stage Dockerfile** with dependency caching and JRE runtime
| MCP:MGLy8W | - **JaCoCo Code Coverage** reports
| MCP:MGLy8W | - **Maven Enforcer** — ensures Java 25+ and Maven 3.8.7+
| MCP:MGLy8W | - **Lombok** and **MapStruct** for reduced boilerplate
| MCP:MGLy8W | - **Environment Isolation** — run dev, test, and prod in parallel via Docker Compose
| MCP:MGLy8W | - **Unit & Integration Tests** for services, controllers, and infrastructure
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | > **Note:** Java 25 and Maven 3.8.7+ are required.
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ---
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ## Quick Start
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ### 1. Use This Template
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | Click **"Use this template"** on GitHub, then clone your new repository:
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ```sh
| MCP:MGLy8W | git clone https://github.com/<your-username>/<your-repo>.git
| MCP:MGLy8W | cd <your-repo>
| MCP:MGLy8W | ```
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ### 2. Initialize Your Project
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | Run the initialization script to rename packages, modules, and containers to match your project:
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ```sh
| MCP:MGLy8W | ./init-project.sh --group-id com.acme --artifact my-app --name "My App" --author "Your Name"
| MCP:MGLy8W | ```
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | | Option | Description | Required |
| MCP:MGLy8W | |--------|-------------|----------|
| MCP:MGLy8W | | `--group-id` | Maven groupId / Java package root (e.g. `com.acme`) | Yes |
| MCP:MGLy8W | | `--artifact` | Artifact name in kebab-case (e.g. `my-app`) | Yes |
| MCP:MGLy8W | | `--name` | Human-readable project name (default: derived from artifact) | No |
| MCP:MGLy8W | | `--author` | Author name for `@author` tags (default: `tzesh`) | No |
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | You can also run it interactively — omit the flags and you'll be prompted:
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ```sh
| MCP:MGLy8W | ./init-project.sh
| MCP:MGLy8W | ```
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | The script will:
| MCP:MGLy8W | - Rename Java packages (`com.tzesh.springtemplate` -> your package)
| MCP:MGLy8W | - Rename module directories (`spring-boot-template-api` -> `<artifact>-api`, etc.)
| MCP:MGLy8W | - Update Docker container names, Kafka group IDs, and all references
| MCP:MGLy8W | - Replace `@author` tags if `--author` is provided
| MCP:MGLy8W | - Self-delete after completion
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ### 3. Verify the setup
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ```sh
| MCP:MGLy8W | ./mvnw clean install
| MCP:MGLy8W | ```
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ### 4. Start infrastructure and run
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ```sh
| MCP:MGLy8W | # Start PostgreSQL, Redis, Kafka
| MCP:MGLy8W | docker compose up -d db redis zookeeper kafka
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | # Run the application
| MCP:MGLy8W | ./mvnw -pl spring-boot-template-api spring-boot:run
| MCP:MGLy8W | ```
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | Or run everything with Docker:
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ```sh
| MCP:MGLy8W | docker compose up -d
| MCP:MGLy8W | ```
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | The application will be available at [http://localhost:8080/api/v1/swagger-ui/index.html](http://localhost:8080/api/v1/swagger-ui/index.html).
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ---
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ## Project Structure
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ```
| MCP:MGLy8W | SpringBootTemplate/
| MCP:MGLy8W | ├── pom.xml                          # Parent POM (enforcer, JaCoCo, shared deps)
| MCP:MGLy8W | ├── spring-boot-template-core/       # Core module (base classes, annotations, exceptions)
| MCP:MGLy8W | │   └── src/main/java/.../base/
| MCP:MGLy8W | │       ├── annotation/              # @RateLimit, @Idempotent, etc.
| MCP:MGLy8W | │       ├── dto/                     # BaseDTO
| MCP:MGLy8W | │       ├── entity/                  # BaseEntity
| MCP:MGLy8W | │       ├── exception/               # NotFoundException, RateLimitExceededException, etc.
| MCP:MGLy8W | │       ├── handler/                 # @ControllerAdvice exception handlers
| MCP:MGLy8W | │       ├── mapper/                  # BaseMapper (MapStruct)
| MCP:MGLy8W | │       ├── response/                # BaseResponse wrapper
| MCP:MGLy8W | │       └── service/                 # BaseService (CRUD + pagination)
| MCP:MGLy8W | ├── spring-boot-template-api/        # API module (controllers, services, config)
| MCP:MGLy8W | │   ├── Dockerfile                   # Multi-stage build
| MCP:MGLy8W | │   └── src/main/java/.../
| MCP:MGLy8W | │       ├── config/
| MCP:MGLy8W | │       │   ├── correlation/         # CorrelationIdFilter
| MCP:MGLy8W | │       │   ├── idempotency/         # IdempotencyAspect
| MCP:MGLy8W | │       │   ├── ratelimit/           # RateLimitAspect, RateLimitingFilter
| MCP:MGLy8W | │       │   └── security/            # SecurityChain, JWT filters
| MCP:MGLy8W | │       ├── controller/              # REST controllers
| MCP:MGLy8W | │       └── service/                 # Business services
| MCP:MGLy8W | ├── docker-compose.yml               # PostgreSQL, Redis, Kafka, app
| MCP:MGLy8W | ├── init-project.sh                  # Project initialization script
| MCP:MGLy8W | ├── .editorconfig                    # Editor formatting rules
| MCP:MGLy8W | ├── .gitattributes                   # Git line ending config
| MCP:MGLy8W | └── .dockerignore                    # Docker build exclusions
| MCP:MGLy8W | ```
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ---
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ## API Usage
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | 1. **Register** a new user via `POST /auth/register`
| MCP:MGLy8W | 2. **Login** via `POST /auth/login` to obtain a JWT token
| MCP:MGLy8W | 3. **Authorize** in Swagger UI using the `Authorize` button
| MCP:MGLy8W | 4. **Access** secured endpoints (e.g. user management requires `ADMIN` role)
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ---
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ## Actuator Endpoints
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | Health, info, and metrics are exposed at:
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | | Endpoint | Description |
| MCP:MGLy8W | |----------|-------------|
| MCP:MGLy8W | | `GET /api/v1/actuator/health` | Application health status |
| MCP:MGLy8W | | `GET /api/v1/actuator/info` | Application info |
| MCP:MGLy8W | | `GET /api/v1/actuator/metrics` | Application metrics |
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | Health details are shown when authenticated with an authorized role. Kubernetes liveness and readiness probes are enabled.
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ---
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ## Correlation ID
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | Every request is assigned a correlation ID for distributed tracing:
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | - If the client sends an `X-Correlation-ID` header, it is reused
| MCP:MGLy8W | - Otherwise, a UUID is generated automatically
| MCP:MGLy8W | - The correlation ID is added to the MDC for all log output and returned in the response header
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ---
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ## Rate Limiting
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ### Annotation-based (per-endpoint)
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ```java
| MCP:MGLy8W | @RateLimit(limit = 20, duration = 1, key = RateLimitKeyStrategy.USER)
| MCP:MGLy8W | @RateLimitCategory(RateLimitCategoryType.STANDARD)
| MCP:MGLy8W | ```
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ### Configurable categories in `application.properties`
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | | Category | Default Limit | Duration |
| MCP:MGLy8W | |----------|--------------|----------|
| MCP:MGLy8W | | `strict` | 10 req | 1 min |
| MCP:MGLy8W | | `standard` | 60 req | 1 min |
| MCP:MGLy8W | | `relaxed` | 200 req | 1 min |
| MCP:MGLy8W | | `authentication` | 5 req | 1 min |
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ---
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ## Idempotency
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | Annotate mutating endpoints with `@Idempotent` to prevent duplicate processing. Clients must send an `Idempotency-Key` header. Backed by Redis.
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ```java
| MCP:MGLy8W | @PostMapping("/")
| MCP:MGLy8W | @Idempotent
| MCP:MGLy8W | public ResponseEntity<BaseResponse<UserDTO>> createUser(...) { ... }
| MCP:MGLy8W | ```
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ---
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ## Kafka Messaging
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | - Kafka and Zookeeper are included in Docker Compose
| MCP:MGLy8W | - `POST /kafka/send` sends messages to Kafka (supports optional scheduled delivery)
| MCP:MGLy8W | - Consumer logs received messages
| MCP:MGLy8W | - Logging level configurable in `application.properties`
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ---
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ## Environment Configuration
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | Use `.env` files for environment-specific variables and Docker Compose override files for port/resource separation:
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ```sh
| MCP:MGLy8W | # Run test environment
| MCP:MGLy8W | docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.test.override.yml --env-file .env.test -p myapp_test up -d
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | # Run prod environment
| MCP:MGLy8W | docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.prod.override.yml --env-file .env.prod -p myapp_prod up -d
| MCP:MGLy8W | ```
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ---
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ## Code Coverage
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | JaCoCo reports are generated during `mvn test` and available at:
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ```
| MCP:MGLy8W | spring-boot-template-api/target/site/jacoco/index.html
| MCP:MGLy8W | spring-boot-template-core/target/site/jacoco/index.html
| MCP:MGLy8W | ```
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ---
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ## Contributing
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | See [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) for setup instructions, code style, and PR process.
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ---
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ## License
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | See [LICENSE](LICENSE) for details.

| MCP:MGLy8W | # Stage 1: Build
| MCP:MGLy8W | FROM eclipse-temurin:25-jdk AS builder
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | WORKDIR /build
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | # Copy Maven wrapper and config first (dependency cache layer)
| MCP:MGLy8W | COPY mvnw .
| MCP:MGLy8W | COPY .mvn .mvn
| MCP:MGLy8W | COPY pom.xml .
| MCP:MGLy8W | COPY spring-boot-template-core/pom.xml spring-boot-template-core/pom.xml
| MCP:MGLy8W | COPY spring-boot-template-api/pom.xml spring-boot-template-api/pom.xml
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | RUN chmod +x mvnw && ./mvnw dependency:go-offline -B
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | # Copy source code
| MCP:MGLy8W | COPY spring-boot-template-core/src spring-boot-template-core/src
| MCP:MGLy8W | COPY spring-boot-template-api/src spring-boot-template-api/src
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | # Build the API module (and its dependency core module)
| MCP:MGLy8W | RUN ./mvnw package -pl spring-boot-template-api -am -DskipTests -B
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | # Stage 2: Runtime
| MCP:MGLy8W | FROM eclipse-temurin:25-jre
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | WORKDIR /app
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | COPY --from=builder /build/spring-boot-template-api/target/spring-boot-template-api-*.jar app.jar
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | EXPOSE 8080
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ENV JAVA_OPTS=""
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=5s --start-period=30s --retries=3 \
| MCP:MGLy8W |   CMD curl -f http://localhost:8080/api/v1/actuator/health || exit 1
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ENTRYPOINT ["sh", "-c", "java $JAVA_OPTS -jar app.jar"]

data.content: Now I have enough information to provide a comprehensive answer. Let me also check if there are any Docker Compose override files mentioned in the README documentation:

content.text: | MCP:MGLy8W | successfully downloaded text file (SHA: 05ea58fff3685455fde9c85dd05f305e8ec02b09)

| MCP:MGLy8W | # Contributing to Spring Boot Template
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | Thank you for your interest in contributing! This guide will help you get started.
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ## Prerequisites
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | - **Java 25** (Eclipse Temurin recommended)
| MCP:MGLy8W | - **Maven 3.8.7+** (or use the included `./mvnw` wrapper)
| MCP:MGLy8W | - **Docker & Docker Compose** (for running PostgreSQL, Redis, and Kafka locally)
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ## Setup
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | 1. **Clone the repository:**
| MCP:MGLy8W |    ```bash
| MCP:MGLy8W |    git clone https://github.com/Tzesh/SpringBootTemplate.git
| MCP:MGLy8W |    cd SpringBootTemplate
| MCP:MGLy8W |    ```
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | 2. **Start infrastructure services:**
| MCP:MGLy8W |    ```bash
| MCP:MGLy8W |    docker compose up -d db redis zookeeper kafka
| MCP:MGLy8W |    ```
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | 3. **Build the project:**
| MCP:MGLy8W |    ```bash
| MCP:MGLy8W |    ./mvnw clean install
| MCP:MGLy8W |    ```
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | 4. **Run the application:**
| MCP:MGLy8W |    ```bash
| MCP:MGLy8W |    ./mvnw -pl spring-boot-template-api spring-boot:run
| MCP:MGLy8W |    ```
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ## Code Style
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | - **Indentation:** 4 spaces (no tabs)
| MCP:MGLy8W | - **Line endings:** LF (Unix-style)
| MCP:MGLy8W | - **Charset:** UTF-8
| MCP:MGLy8W | - Use Lombok annotations where appropriate (`@RequiredArgsConstructor`, `@Getter`, etc.)
| MCP:MGLy8W | - Follow existing patterns in `BaseService`, `BaseEntity`, and `BaseDTO`
| MCP:MGLy8W | - Keep controllers thin — business logic belongs in services
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ## Branching
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | - `master` — stable, release-ready code
| MCP:MGLy8W | - `feature/<name>` — new features
| MCP:MGLy8W | - `fix/<name>` — bug fixes
| MCP:MGLy8W | - `release-<version>` — release branches
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ## Pull Request Process
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | 1. Create a feature or fix branch from `master`
| MCP:MGLy8W | 2. Make your changes with clear, focused commits
| MCP:MGLy8W | 3. Ensure all tests pass: `./mvnw clean verify`
| MCP:MGLy8W | 4. Open a PR against `master`
| MCP:MGLy8W | 5. Provide a clear description of your changes
| MCP:MGLy8W | 6. Wait for review and address feedback
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ## Testing
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | - Write unit tests for new services and controllers
| MCP:MGLy8W | - Place tests in the corresponding `src/test/java` directory
| MCP:MGLy8W | - Run tests: `./mvnw test`
| MCP:MGLy8W | - Check code coverage reports in `target/site/jacoco/`
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | ## Questions?
| MCP:MGLy8W | 
| MCP:MGLy8W | Open an issue on GitHub if you have questions or need help.

data.content:
Based on my examination of the repository, here's how environment-specific execution is documented in this Spring Boot template:

## Infrastructure Services Started with Docker Compose

The `docker-compose.yml` defines **5 infrastructure services**:

1. **db** - PostgreSQL 16 (`postgres:16`)
   - Container: `spring_template_postgres`
   - Default port: 5432
   - Persistent volume: `postgres_data`

2. **app** - Spring Boot Application
   - Built from `spring-boot-template-api/Dockerfile`
   - Container: `spring_boot_template_api`
   - Default port: 8080
   - Depends on: db, redis

3. **redis** - Redis 7 (`redis:7`)
   - Container: `spring_template_redis`
   - Default port: 6379

4. **zookeeper** - Confluent Zookeeper (`confluentinc/cp-zookeeper:7.5.0`)
   - Container: `zookeeper`
   - Default port: 2181

5. **kafka** - Confluent Kafka (`confluentinc/cp-kafka:7.5.0`)
   - Container: `kafka`
   - Default port: 9092
   - Depends on: zookeeper

## Environment-Specific Configuration

The template uses **environment-specific `.env` files** to distinguish environments:

| Environment | File | Spring Profile | Database Name | DB User |
|-------------|------|----------------|---------------|---------|
| Development | `.env.dev` | `dev` | `spring_template_dev` | `development` |
| Test | `.env.test` | `test` | `spring_template_test` | `test` |
| QA | `.env.qa` | `qa` | `spring_template_qa` | `qa` |
| Production | `.env.prod` | `prod` | `spring_template_prod` | `production` |
| Default | `.env` | `dev` | `spring_template_dev` | `development` |

## How Test and Prod Environments Are Distinguished

According to the README's **Environment Configuration** section:

1. **Environment Isolation**: The template supports running dev, test, and prod **in parallel** via Docker Compose

2. **Docker Compose Override Files**: The documentation mentions using override files for port/resource separation:
   - `docker-compose.test.override.yml` for test
   - `docker-compose.prod.override.yml` for prod

3. **Commands for Running Different Environments**:
   ```sh
   # Run test environment
   docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.test.override.yml --env-file .env.test -p myapp_test up -d
   
   # Run prod environment
   docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.prod.override.yml --env-file .env.prod -p myapp_prod up -d
   ```

4. **Key Differentiators**:
   - Different Spring profiles (`dev`, `test`, `qa`, `prod`) via `SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE`
   - Isolated PostgreSQL databases per environment
   - Different container names (via `-p` project flag) to run instances in parallel
   - Separate Docker Compose override files for port/resource separation

## Notable Observation

While the README documents the pattern for multi-environment support, the repository currently **does not include** the Docker Compose override files (`docker-compose.test.override.yml` and `docker-compose.prod.override.yml`) that are referenced in the documentation. The base `docker-compose.yml` uses environment variable defaults, and the `.env` files provide environment-specific values that can be injected via the `--env-file` flag.
